description:
⚡ Push code. Get a live URL. No servers. That's Vercel. 🚀

Vercel explained from scratch — how a simple Git-push deployment platform became an AI Cloud for modern web applications. Whether you're a beginner deploying your first Next.js app or an engineer evaluating Vercel against Netlify, Cloudflare, or AWS, this video covers everything you need to know.

🎯 What you'll learn
- How Vercel's Git-based deployment and preview URLs work
- Why Next.js and Vercel are built by the same team — and what that means
- What v0, AI SDK, AI Gateway, and Fluid Compute actually do
- How Fluid Compute differs from traditional serverless for AI workloads
- What Vercel's $9.3B valuation tells you about its direction
- Honest trade-offs: vendor lock-in, pricing surprises, and security responsibility


🔗 Links
- Vercel: https://vercel.com
- Vercel Pricing: https://vercel.com/pricing
- v0: https://v0.dev
- Vercel AI SDK: https://sdk.vercel.ai
- Vercel AI Gateway Docs: https://vercel.com/docs/ai-gateway
- Fluid Compute Docs: https://vercel.com/docs/fluid-compute
- Next.js: https://nextjs.org

Vercel explained simply: it's a platform that takes your Git repo and turns every push into a live, globally distributed URL — with no server to configure. What makes it more interesting in 2026 is the AI layer on top: v0 for natural-language app generation, the AI SDK for building streaming chat interfaces, AI Gateway for routing model traffic, and Fluid Compute for running long-running AI workloads. If you're comparing vercel vs netlify or vercel next.js deployment options, this video gives you the full picture including pricing, lock-in risk, and enterprise features.

#vercel #nextjs #webdevelopment
#webdev #aitools
#Shorts
fulltitle: Vercel's AI Cloud Actually Changes Everything | v0, AI SDK, Fluid Compute #Shorts
